blurb: Bologna's pride — slow-cooked meat ragù, creamy béchamel, fresh pasta layered and baked.

prov.popular_in: Italian households and Sunday dinner tables globally
prov.famous_for: The classic lasagna with ragù Bolognese and béchamel (no ricotta!)
prov.consists_of: Fresh pasta sheets layered with slow-cooked beef-pork ragù and creamy béchamel, baked until bubbly
prov.name_origin: Lasagne alla Bolognese — from Bologna, Emilia-Romagna; traditional lasagne predates tomatoes
prov.why_loved: The combination of rich ragù, silky béchamel, and tender pasta layers is Italian comfort at its finest
prov.method_detail: Oven — ragù simmered 2+ hours, béchamel made, pasta layered (ragù, béchamel, parmigiano), baked 40-45 min, rested 15 min

brief.lead: Build the classic lasagne alla Bolognese — a labour of love that rewards patience with deeply layered flavour. Start with a slow-cooked beef-pork ragù, craft a silky béchamel, then layer with fresh pasta and generous Parmigiano.
expect: Ragù simmers for 2+ hours to develop depth
Make ragù, béchamel, and assembly in sequence
Essential for clean slices and set texture

tl: Sauté vegetables, brown meats, simmer 2+ hours
Roux technique, whisk in warm milk
steps: Build the soffritto
Sauté onion, carrot, celery in olive oil 8-10 min until soft. Add garlic, cook 1 min.
Foundational flavour base for authentic ragù.
Brown the meats
Add beef and pork mince, brown well 8-10 min. Break up clumps, evaporate moisture.
Maillard reaction develops deep savoury depth.
Deglaze and simmer
Add red wine, reduce 2 min. Stir in tomato purée, stock, milk, oregano. Simmer gently 90 min, stirring occasionally.
Wine adds acidity; long simmer melds flavours.
Make the béchamel
Melt butter, stir in flour, cook 2 min. Whisk in warm milk gradually, simmer 5-8 min until thick. Season with salt, nutmeg, pepper.
Silky sauce layers bind the lasagne.
Assemble first layers
Spread ragù on dish bottom. Layer pasta, béchamel, Parmigiano. Repeat 3 more times (ragù, pasta, béchamel, cheese).
Even layers ensure consistent cooking.
Finish and bake
Top with final pasta, remaining béchamel, generous Parmigiano, butter cubes. Bake 40-45 min at 190°C until golden and bubbling.
Cheese and butter create golden crust.
Rest before serving
Cool 15 min uncovered. Cut squares with sharp knife.
Resting sets structure for clean slices.

blurb: France's brunch classic — buttery pastry with rich bacon-Gruyère custard.

prov.popular_in: French bistros and brunch tables globally
prov.famous_for: The classic French quiche with bacon and Gruyère in rich egg-cream custard
prov.consists_of: Buttery pastry crust filled with bacon lardons, Gruyère cheese, and eggs-cream custard, baked
prov.name_origin: From Lorraine region in northeast France; originally without cheese (purist version)
prov.why_loved: The silky-smooth custard, smoky bacon, and nutty Gruyère in flaky pastry is brunch perfection
prov.method_detail: Oven — pastry shell blind-baked, bacon lardons crisped, custard (eggs, cream, milk, cheese) poured, baked 35-45 min until just set

brief.lead: Master the French quiche Lorraine — buttery pâte brisée shell filled with smoky bacon lardons, nutty Gruyère, and silken egg-cream custard. Blind-bake the crust for flaky perfection, then bake until just set with a slight wobble.

tl: Rub butter into flour, rest 30 min chilled
Line with foil, beans, bake 20 min
Whisk eggs, cream, milk, spices, cheese
Layer bacon, pour custard, bake 35-45 min
steps: Make pâte brisée
Rub cold butter into flour and salt until crumb-like. Add ice water, bring together gently. Wrap, chill 30 min.
Cold butter creates flaky layers.
Line the tin
Roll dough 3mm thick, line tart tin, trim edges. Chill 15 min.
Chilling prevents shrinking during baking.
Blind-bake crust
Prick base, line with foil and baking beans. Bake 20 min at 180°C. Remove foil, brush with egg wash, bake 5 min.
Ensures crisp, fully-cooked pastry base.
Crisp the bacon
Cook lardons in dry pan 6-8 min until golden and fat rendered. Drain on paper.
Rendering removes excess fat, concentrates flavour.
Make custard
Whisk eggs, cream, milk, salt, pepper, nutmeg until smooth. Stir in 80g Gruyère, bacon lardons.
Egg-cream ratio yields silky texture.
Assemble and bake
Place remaining Gruyère on crust base. Pour in custard. Bake 35-45 min at 160°C until just set with slight wobble.
Gentle bake prevents curdling and cracking.
Rest and serve
Cool 10 min before slicing. Serve warm or room temperature.
Resting allows custard to set completely.

blurb: Greece's spinach pie — crisp phyllo layers with spinach, feta, and herbs.

prov.popular_in: Greek households and meze tables globally
prov.famous_for: The classic Greek spinach and feta pie in crispy phyllo pastry
prov.consists_of: Spinach cooked with onions, herbs, mixed with feta cheese, wrapped in phyllo, baked until golden
prov.name_origin: Spanaki = 'spinach', pita = 'pie' — traditional Greek mezze dish
prov.why_loved: The crispy phyllo with salty-savoury spinach-feta filling is universally appealing
prov.method_detail: Oven — spinach wilted, drained, mixed with sautéed onions, herbs, feta, layered in phyllo (buttered), baked 35-45 min until golden

brief.lead: Craft authentic spanakopita — butter-brushed phyllo wrapped around spinach, feta, and aromatic herbs. Wilt and drain the spinach thoroughly, mix with sautéed onions and fresh dill, then fold into crisp golden layers.
expect: Spinach must be well-drained to prevent soggy phyllo
Fold bottom over top for rustic finish

tl: Cook onions and garlic in olive oil
Combine spinach, onions, feta, herbs, eggs
steps: Wilt the spinach
Place spinach in large pan, wilt 3-4 min in batches. Cool in colander, squeeze out ALL liquid.
Removing moisture prevents soggy phyllo.
Sauté aromatics
Heat olive oil, cook onions 6-8 min until soft. Add garlic, spring onions, cook 2 min.
Building flavour foundation for filling.
Mix the filling
Chop spinach finely. Combine with onions, feta, eggs, dill, parsley, salt, pepper. Mix well.
Eggs bind; feta provides salty punch.
Prepare phyllo
Melt butter with little olive oil. Keep phyllo covered with damp cloth. Brush 5 sheets with butter, layer on tray.
Damp cloth prevents drying and cracking.
Layer and fold
Spread filling in centre. Fold bottom phyllo over, then sides. Brush and layer 3 more sheets on top, tucking edges.
Rustic fold protects filling during bake.
Score and bake
Brush top with butter. Score into diamonds with sharp knife (don't cut through). Bake 30-40 min at 180°C until golden.
Scoring helps portioning; butter aids browning.
Rest and serve
Cool 5 min. Cut along scored lines into squares or diamonds.
Brief rest sets structure for clean cuts.
